title: quiina longifolia
intro: Quiina longifolia, also known as Quina or Quina-Quina, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. Its health benefits include treating fever, malaria, and other diseases, as well as being used as an anti-inflammatory to soothe pain and reduce swelling. Quiina longifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
